name: Warren Dupre Arthur, Jr.
motto: Three drinks, and the world is mine
clubs: Soph, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
Secretary and Treasurer Soph Dancing Club
description: "Doc," as he is called by all, except one, who calls him "DOCKIE DEAR," has one great weakness—ladies. His winning ways and line of "bull" make him popular among the "fair ones"; while his leadership and character have won him a front place among his fellow-students. "Doc" is some business man, being proprietor of the "Jew Shop." He hasn't decided whether he will get rich in Argentina, or go back to that town with the fitting motto, "Union Suits."

name: Arthur, William Hooker
motto: There is no place like Home, Sweet Home.
description: He came to us from Union, S. C., but soon migrated to Virginia. He spends most of his time at the store. He is determined to be Governor of Virginia; but if he doesn't get this, he says he will take President of the United States. He has gathered around him many friends since coming to Clemson. If he should fail to become President, we know he will revolutionize the apple industry in Virginia. We wish him much success.

name: Byers, James Lafayette
motto: Be not a dreamer among shadows, but a man among men
description: "BIRMIE" is a worthy product of Orangeburg, where he has been greatly missed since his departure in the pursuit of knowledge. "BIRMINGHAM" thought he was a military genius, but the "Col." didn't share his views, and consequently he is a high ranking member of the Clean-Sleeve Club. "JIM" is known to be very truthful and industrious, and we sincerely believe that he will be able to figure out some "route" whereby alcohol may be obtained as a by-product from the manufacture of cotton goods.

name: Cannon, John Clyde
motto: Character is the diamond which scratches every other stone
clubs: Chief of Literary Staff of Tiger
Treasurer of Professor Daniel's Sunday School Class
description: "Kid" is one of Greenville's representatives, and well may she claim him for a fair representative. Because of his reserved, unassuming ways, he was known only to a few intimate friends at first. But his pleasant manners, his high ideals of life, his absolute honesty and integrity, coupled with an abundance of native ability, gradually singled him out from the common class of boys. In the last two years, many honors have been his, and to each he has been conscientiously faithful.

name: Cathcart, Thomas Madden Jr.
motto: Throw away your pipe, your happy pipe, And eat that royal weed.
description: Came to us from historic Winnsboro, in the fall of '11. Since entering Clemson, he has accumulated a vast amount of knowledge, attained high military standing, and caused the "mailman" no end of trouble. He has the "rep" of receiving more mail than any other cadet in College. "KITTY" is a typical ladies' man, a fine athlete, and an all-round good fellow. We wish him all success in the horticultural world.

name: Caughman, Wilbur West
motto: "Of fertile mind, of massive heart, He always acted well his part."
description: Among the noted men who claim Lexington as their "brier-patch," W. W. stands pre-eminent. His good-natured smile, and his general appearance, have won for him many friends among the ladies and his classmates and even to the Colonel. He spends much of his spare time in the foothills of the Blue Ridge (Walhalla). He expects to go back to the sandhills of Lexington, and show the farmers around his home how to grow two "big hogs" where one "little pig" grew before.

name: Causey, Richard Greene
motto: "To thine own self be true, And it must follow, as the night the day, Thou canst not then be false to any man."
description: Honor, loyalty, frankness, fearlessness, and long legs characterize him. These traits have made him a true friend to his friends. He tries everything once; and even tried the "Colonel's Crowd" in Junior, but didn't like it—so he resigned. His marked ability is shown by the admirable record he has made for himself. His one great failing is "Girl." But his wonderful "line" has made his failing a great success, and won lots of love for his "CAUSE—Y."

name: Clark, Joseph Rhett
motto: He giveth his beloved sleep, to carry this mighty Rat
clubs: Sergeant-at-Arms, Literary Critic, Orator, Annual Celebration '15, Columbian Literary Society
description: This great big brown-eyed Chapinonian cast his lot with the '14 Class; but, on account of sickness, he was unable to finish freshman that year, and came back the next year full of determination to be a great Agronomist. His intelligent look has carried him through College, and it will win him a great place in life. Owing to his silver-tongued oratory, this lad might decide to be a great politician, after giving Agronomy a thorough trial.

name: Frank, M. Connor
motto: Behold! This is a man
description: "FRANK," or "SARG," as he is better known, is a pessimist, true and tried. He swears that there is no girl living that he cares for; but we who room with him know better than he does. He thought that he was getting an easy course when he took Agriculture, but was surprised to learn differently when the exams came. We predict a happy life for "SARG," knowing that there is no member of our Class who better deserves it than he does.

name: Corbett, Virgil Payne
motto: Once a meteor; but now a star that will illuminate the eternal pages of history.
description: On a dark and stormy night, left the woods around Horatio for Clemson. Here he startled the world by a military career like unto a meteor—startling in its brilliancy, but dying at its brightest moment. Bright and cheery, like the rest of the gamecock lads, he has the fault of building air castles. The chiefest castle was built on or about Remberts. But—! However, we are assured that Sumter County will ever be proud to say: "This is my son."

name: Percival, Chiswell, Crayton
motto: I will grapple them to my heart with hooks of steel
description: First discovered the light of day in the Electric City. Being ambitious, he strayed away, but later was caught at Clemson, where he was sentenced to serve four years behind the bars. He has skipped through College to the "Jingle of the Silvery Bells." His chief ambition in life is to be Commandant of Frazier's Military Academy. We predict that "Percy" will spend the rest of his life in breaking ladies' hearts, and doffing spinning frames.

name: Creecy, Peter Julian
motto: "Who, me? Why yes, I can grow roses on a sweet potato vine."
description: Well! here comes quite a little chap; and we would say that Clarendon County should look to "His Highness," "Peter," or "P. J." He surprised us with his appearance here in 1911, and has continued to surprise us ever since with his noble intellect. We invariably find his floor carpeted with old scraps of paper, as he never polices his room, for fear of falling into the habit. On visiting him, we are always welcomed with his favorite song, "I Love the Ladies."

name: Barksdale, Ralph Cureton
motto: "RALPH" is the genuine article, a chip off the courteous Southern type of a gentleman. He has made many friends among the cadets. Everywhere he goes, his winning way increases his ever-widening circle of friends. For this type of man, success is assured. He will be a successful civil engineer—"Nothing can keep a good man down." The girl who wins him will possess a valuable prize, love, and assured happiness.

name: Darby, John Thomas
motto: I am sure care is an enemy to life
clubs: Soph, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Possesses three qualities of note—a quick, bright mind; a jolly and lovable disposition; and laziness. His possessions have won for him many things—among them, the esteem of his professors; a fine record; a host of true friends; and a private's misfortune, namely: Extra. His favorite pastime is expounding the use of "Musical Bonbons." His ability, earnest application, and admirable character are sufficient to make a future full of success for him.

name: Wilfert, O'dell Davis
motto:
'Tis better to have loved and lost
Than never to have loved at all
description: He is a product of the swamps of Georgetown. He has won great fame around barracks by telling of his wonderful experiences while hunting ducks and fishing in the swamps around Georgetown. Although he is a quiet, modest chap, he is liked by all who know him. His greatest ambition is to manage a large truck farm. We predict that he will settle down to a quiet life, and make two blades of grass grow where only one grew before.

name: Dukes, Henry Hugh
motto: In stature, small; but every whit a man withal
description: Some thought that the College was robbing the cradle when entered, but his unfailing attendance upon early meetings, and his later decision to become a doctor, have convinced us all that he is indeed a man among men. has prospered as the efficient financier of our Y. M. C. A., and if he does not take his will doubtless enlighten his native town with bright light.

name: Duvernet, Ernest Parker
motto: A little learning is a dangerous thing; Drink deep, or taste not the Pierian spring.
clubs: Executive Committee A. I. E. E.
description: A quiet, serious lad with striking personality, became imbued with a thirst for electrical knowledge. He drank deep of the Pierian spring, as is evidenced by his ability to "shoot." He is a deep thinker, a sound reasoner, and a regular storehouse of information. He has many close friends, who admire his fine qualities. We predict that in the future we shall hear of his great work as a consulting engineer of the General Electric Company.

name: Montrose, Edmonds
motto: I see, and strive to reach the height That lies forever in the light
description: He entered College in 1911, and has remained loyal for four long years. Agriculture was his first choice, but in his Sophomore year he saw his mistake, and fell in line with the Electricals. He may not be a Steinmetz, but is going to be proud of him. record is one to be proud of, and we predict for him a bright future in the electrical world.

name: Evans, David William
motto: Nothing in his College course became him like the leaving it
description: Left the city of Cameron in 1911, joined the ranks of the Clemsonites, and since that time has been a faithful member of the "Hutch-Lovers." It is reported that he is also something of a ladies' man, and his frequent trips to the hotel serve in no way to contradict this assumption. However, in spite of this, he stands well in his classes and is sure to end up O. K.

name: Folk, Chris Edwin
motto: Grasp every opportunity as it approaches us.
description: He is specializing on the "dyeing" phase of the textile industry, and can give valuable information about this and all stages beyond. He has always made good at everything, making numerous friends at College and among the fair sex. Nothing but success can be predicted for "Miss" Folk, for his motto is: "Never work in single harness, but rather in double."

name: Foster, James Carlisle
motto: "What is so fair as a radiant maiden, Or so rare as love divine?"
description: He is very proud of his old home, White Stone, S. C. He has a large number of distinguishing characteristics, the chief of which is his ability to write fluent love-letters. The interest that he takes in English will eventually insure him complete success. He is taking the Agronomy course, and it is just possible that he will teach in the newly erected agricultural high school at White Stone, S. C.

name: Garris, Edward Walter
motto: Let what will be, be
description: "Ned" was one of the first "Rats" to enter barracks in 1911. Before coming to Clemson he resolved to make good. This resolution presumably had a good effect, for he now has a quite enviable record. At present he is running experiments to see if he can grow apples on cucumber vines. "Ned" is modest, hardworking, and wise; his ability to grasp ideas is wonderful. These qualities will undoubtedly make his future brilliant.

name: Barker, Henry D.
motto: Success doesn't come to a man, but out of him
clubs: Presiding Officer, Annual Celebration of Palmetto Literary Society
description: He came here along with the rest of us, but since then he has forged ahead of us. A leader in whatever required work and faithful application. He is a serious-minded, precocious chap, speaking only when he has something to say. His chief delight is to shoot all his professors for "Block-Hundreds"; his pastime is quoting poetry. He must be in love. It is whispered that his favorite fruit is the Georgia "Belle" Peach. His chief aim while here seems to be "Do It, Do It Right, Do It Right Now."

name: Barnes, Francis Sanders
motto: A man's a man for a' that
description: This, ladies and gentlemen, is the engineer of our Class. His cartoon will show what he can do with this instrument. He has one very bad habit, that of making hundreds on all exams. It is often said that when he enters an exam. room the Prof. trembles, because someone is going to get "shot." Too much cannot be said of his ability, and we expect great things of him after graduation.

name: Clough, Farrer Gee
motto: We know what we are, but know not what we may be
clubs: Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Wandered up this way from the Capital City in '11. Since then he has studied some, played football some more, and made a host of friends by his frank good nature. He never tells anyone why he came to Clemson, but we can make a shrewd guess that it was on account of a love affair. At present, "Muttr" is undecided whether to cast his lot with the engineers, coach a football team, or teach a dancing school.

name: Glover, John Elliott Jr.
motto: I have no words, My tongue is in my sword.
description: He joined our ranks in Soph, and soon became known for his military aspirations and accomplishments. His mandolin and voice soon landed him among the songsters, and a mention of a Glee Club date with Winthrop sets his heart a-flutter. As a graceful dancer, he scarcely has an equal. We wish him the greatest success in his chosen profession of "Wire-Stringer."

name: Hamilton, Ben Lee
motto: "When life's all love, 'tis life; aught else, 'tis naught"
description: He is not to be found wanting in energy in anything he undertakes, and will make his way in the world. He has a Statewide reputation as a heart-smasher. All of his dates are "figured out till June"; then he will open a garage in Seneca, Atlanta, or some other small town, and make a success.

name: Harris, George Lawrence
motto: May we look forward with pleasure, and backward without regret
description: He delights in telling of the "moonshine" around this particular cave. His fellow-students all admire him, because of his kind disposition. "Gawge" dotes on reading love stories, and says that the height of his ambition is to make one of these love stories real. "On to the Philippines" often strikes into his mind, but he consoles himself by saying, "A hoss, a dog, and a pretty little wife is home, sweet home to me."

motto: He draweth out the thread of his verbosity finer than the staple of his argument
clubs: Reporting Critic, Censor Palmetto Literary Society
Secretary and Treasurer Fairfield County Club '12-'13
Senior Preps of the Class of '10-'11
Y. M. C. A. Finance Committee '14-'15
description: Descended from the far-famed old brier-patch of Fairfield. No-one knows why he came to Clemson, or at what date he entered. However, it is commonly thought that he was a Prep Rat when King was a Sophomore. G. P. has ever been a worthy pursuant after all sciences, especially Military Science. He will make his future home in one of his many beautiful air castles near Blythewood, where it is rumored a Fair One awaits him.

name: Hopkins, David Ramsay
motto: He is in deep sympathy with mankind; also womankind.
clubs: Corresponding Secretary, Treasurer, Literary Critic Calhoun Literary Society
description: This is a fair representative of Fountain Inn. He came to Clemson with the fixed purpose of making a scientific farmer of himself, but has changed his mind, and is now a member of the Senior "Bulls." He contemplates going to the Philippines, and teaching the natives stock raising. He is the truest of friends, is loved and respected by all, and is a man who will stand by what is right. We feel sure of his future success.

motto: And still the wonder went around and grew, How one small head could carry all he— didn't know
description: Hails from the city of Greenville. When this lad entered College, neither his size nor manner caused anyone to fear him. Soon became known as a quiet, jolly, happy-go-lucky kind of fellow, who was ever ready to help others out of difficulties. Although talented as an artist, this ambitious youth has decided upon scratching soils for the United States. We predict for him such success as will repay Greenville for having spared him four years.

Hails from the city of Greenville. When this lad entered College, neither his size nor manner caused anyone to fear him. Soon became known as a quiet, jolly, happy-go-lucky kind of fellow, who was ever ready to help others out of difficulties. Although talented as an artist, this ambitious youth has decided upon scratching soils for the United States. We predict for him such success as will repay Greenville for having spared him four years.

name: Hunter, William Joseph
motto: "A lover of the meadows and the woods, And mountains; and of all that we behold From the green earth; of all the mighty world Of eye and ear."
clubs: P. L. S. Delegate to S. C. C. P. A. '14, '15
First Vice-President S. C. C. P. A. '15
Executive Committeeman S. C. C. P. A. '15
description: This is he, and may Liberty be proud of her son. He is a man of achievement as well as of words. Calmly, with poetry as his constant companion, he sails life's sea. With a loving disposition, and a kind heart, he has made a large circle of friends. Popular he is: popular he will always be. Honest, upright, and sincere in all his dealings, he is ordained for a success in life. Either the literary or the textile world shall hear from him some day.

name: Iler, Claude Bartow
motto: May the corners of your mouth never turn down
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Junior Electrical Science Club '14
description: "Pip" is a product of the great school of diplomats. Since his first entrance into College, his shining accomplishment has been his ability to get what he wants by his persuasive manner and his jovial good nature. As a baseball player, "Pip" has made a most creditable showing. He is one of the most popular men in our Class, as is shown by the many honors bestowed upon him; and he will always be remembered as the originator of the famous "Yabbo" yell.

name: Jeffords, Thomas Eugene
motto: "Come, let us go while we are in our prime; And take the harmless follies of the time."
description: This lad has, through his jolly and congenial disposition, made a host of friends. This quality, coupled with his earnest application, will be sure to spell success in life. His highest ambition is to be the architect of Darlington's future skyscraper, which is to have the top story hinged, in order to allow the moon to move on in its journey undisturbed.

name: Jenkins, Robert Flavel
motto: He thinks too little, but talks too much
description: "Jock-O" is a fair representative of Florence. He has made himself famous growing cotton as a trucking crop in the Pee Dee section of Florence. "Jock-O" is a hard-working boy, and one who will succeed in life. He expects to return to Florence, and revolutionize the trucking industry of that county. "Jock-O" has high military aspirations—so high that he has been able to attain but one of them. His military achievements rise and fall.

name: Jennings, Thomas Atmar
motto: "He stands on the heights of his life, with a glimpse of a height that is higher."
description: A tall, broad-shouldered athlete, with pleasant manner and attractive personality, has distinguished himself not only on the athletic field, but in the classroom as well. He claims he does not care for the ladies, but he spends many precious hours writing lengthy epistles to Lander College. He has always been fond of livestock, and is taking the veterinary course; so we expect to hear from when he gets his "D. V. M." at Ohio State University.

name: Johnson, Richard Hughes
motto: "The noblest mind the best contentment has"
description: is a Union County product, and one of whom she should be proud. Since coming to College, in the fall of '11, he has made himself famous as a "feeder of men." "RASTUS," like many of us, has learned to take a deep interest in the affairs at Winthrop. He must hear at least twice a week how the dear College is progressing. However, he is a hard worker, and we bespeak for him great success.

name: Jones, John Dargan
motto: "His pensive cheek upon his hand reclin'd, Anxious thoughts revolving in his mind"
description: He is a typical Gamecock lad. By spirit and determination, along with other aims, he has shown his true self. In his work and making of friends he has been most successful; and, but for his favorite calling, it can be said of him: "Well Done." A follower of Swine's he is; and a worshiper of his beloved Math he has been. In this we predict success.

name: Kennedy, Ralph Guy
motto: Like a can tied to a dog's tail, I raise a hell of a racket.
clubs: Corresponding Secretary, Treasurer, President, Presiding Officer Annual Celebration Columbian Literary Society
description: Here's the guy that put the "bo" in "Hobo." He made many "HITS" in his career, as the unfortunate "Rats" well remember. This feature of his amusement was cut short by the "Col." His great aspirations could not be dampened, so he came back with redoubled determination. He is best known by, and admired for, his constancy and jovial disposition. His great studiousness and ability will win for him a Chief Engineer's position in the Army.

name: Ebenezer Grinnell Kittles
motto: Many a fresh young lily has faded before my enamored gaze
description: Hails from Brighton, S. C. His name is a derivative of the word kettle. Has many accomplishments, the chief of which is his ability to present a ring to a young lady in the most acceptable way. He is essentially a flirt; for he has broken many girls' hearts. He is taking the Civil Engineering course, but indications point to his becoming a successful Minister.

name: Lachicotte, Eugene Stokes
motto: Eat, drink, and be merry, for tomorrow you may die.
description: Here is another product of the swamps of Georgetown. is well known for his fish and duck stories. is also known as an expert ladies' man (?), although he had to get another fellow to find him a sponsor. has numerous friends here, and is liked by all who know him. We know he will make a factory town out of "Waccamaw Neck" if he doesn't desert his profession for hunting and fishing. We all wish him a long, happy life.

name: Johnston, Glenn
motto: "And all may do what has by man been done."
description: Timmonsville should be proud of her Clemson boy, for he has an ambition that will some day make him a famous veterinarian. He is very popular with his classmates, and is liked by all who know him. Although is studying the aches and pains of all domestic animals, his specialty is "chickens." He is also studying the merits of Militarism and Woman Suffrage with great zeal. Fortunate will be the suffragette who lands him.

name: Lawson, Claude Sims
motto: "Happy am I, from care I am free—Why aren't they all contented like me?"
description: One of the youngest members of our Class, "Hoboed" to Clemson in the Fall of 1911. His military aspirations were not very high, as he was contented with a Color Guard. He has a great reputation with "Dicky" and his other professors. Despite his tender years, his record will compare favorably with the best. His heart is where his treasure is—in Charleston, whence come such frequent letters. We predict for "Cutie" a very bright future as a chemist.

name: Legette, Francis Clinton
motto: With honest pride, I scorn each selfish end—My dearest meed, a friend's esteem and praise.
clubs: Literary Critic, Janitor's Committee, Marshal Annual Celebration Columbian Literary Society
description: His striking personality, good nature, and good looks have won for him many friends. Being of a literary turn, he has done much for the advancement of College publications. But this is not his only accomplishment. He prides himself on his mastery of English, and when he becomes a dignified professor of that subject, he will have reached the height of his ambition.

name: Barnett, David Eugene
motto: "If I were a lightning-bug, I would make it my life's work the best of all lightning bugs to be."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Veterinary Science Club
description: "Gene," as he is commonly known, hails from Laurens, S. C. Besides being long, lean, and lanky, he is quite an athlete and ladies' man. He has won fame as a stump speaker for Cole Blease, among showers of chalk and erasers. His time is spent between studying and training his pompadour in the way it should go. Yet, "Gene" is a diligent student, and we predict for him future success in his chosen profession of medicine.

name: Barnett, Marshall Stowe
motto: Few lips would be moved to song if they could find a sufficiency of kissing
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer York County Club, '14
Duke of the House of York, '13
Private: Private: Private: Senior Private: Picked Company, '12, '13
description: He is an independent lad, noted for being an extremely pleasant carefree fellow. If there is any fun stirring anywhere, "PREP" may be counted on as one of the ringleaders. And if he isn't tempted into a matrimonial career Clover will shortly be illuminated.

name: Legrand, Leon
motto: "If music be the food of love, play on"
clubs: Winner of Southern Intercollegiate Architectural Competition
description: "IKEY" began developing his voice in Summer, and was (as his neighbors will vouch) very precocious along this line. "Boot" soon outgrew his native "brier-patch," and came to Clemson in search of a "Block C." Having so little trouble with his feet on the cinder-path, he tried the ballroom, and soon proved that he was born graceful, and hadn't recovered. "Consulting Architect," we are sure, will be one of his minor achievements.

name: Lunney, George Washington
motto: "And when a lady's in the case, You know all other things give place"
description: No, "GEORGE" is not a dreamer, but a practical engineer. As a draftsman and designer, "GEORGE" is to be envied; but do not conclude that he spends all of his time studying. "GEORGE" is attracted by the fair sex, and is accused of monopolizing Seneca society. Be this true or not, we know that many a girl has been unable to resist his pleasant smiles and ever-ready humor. If "GEORGE" decides to make his home in Seneca, the General Electrical Company will lose a valuable man.

name: Magill, William Kennedy
motto: "Some are born great, some achieve greatness, and some have greatness thrust upon them."
description: "Rummy," as he is known to us, is one of the liveliest men in his Class. He hails from the city of Abbeville, though he was born and raised in "Sticks." A big, simple, kindly soul; giant in stature; a little slow, but everlastingly there. His football career speaks for itself. He will hold down Clemson's captaincy in 1915. "Rummy's" ambition is to be head coach at Harvard.

name: Marchant, John Luther
motto: "Here's to those who love us well; Those who don't, can go to —"
description: Hails from the metropolis of Greer, and is noted for his poetic and musical talent (?). Soon after entering College, he fell in love, and since then most of his time has been spent in writing to a member of the fair sex. In his Sophomore year he entered the grocery business, but was soon discovered by the detectives and declared bankrupt. "John's" greatest ambition is to be at the head of some cotton mill; and we wish him the best of success.

name: Martin, George Dean
motto: "Eat, drink, and be merry, for tomorrow you may be married."
description: This carefree, good-natured Senior is the product of Cowpens. He is noted for his fearless, open speeches and frank nature. His favorite pastime is eating peanuts while walking extras. He is a great favorite among the "fair sex," and his unvarying excuse for an absence is, "I was out calling last night." He does not let studying interfere with his College course. Honest, apt, distinguished in bearing, he keeps pace with the fastest strides. We predict a great future for him.

name: McMahon, Edgar Oliver
motto: Speak well of everyone, if you speak of them at all—no one of us is so very good.
description: He is one of Oconee County's best products. He is interested in a literary course and, by special permission from his favorite professor, has obtained special training in English. In taking this course he hoped to attain the degree of "B. S." with which he could bluff a certain little maiden at Winthrop; but before he could get his degree the maiden had sought refuge in matrimony. He is sincere, frank, and ever ready to help anyone who is in need of help.

name: Brunson, S. C. John Miley
motto: He who fights and runs away will live to fight another day
clubs: Marshal Annual Celebration, Columbian Literary Society '15
description: He is a very cheerfully disposed creation, who takes life as it comes. He has certain principles which he strictly adheres to, and which he will fight for if necessary. John is strictly a "ladies' man." He receives no less than a dozen letters a week, and his postage bill alone for one year amounts to $59.00. He will probably be the future leader of Animal Industry in this State.

name: Mitchell, Boyce Spurgeon
motto: I may be a 'long-fellow,' but I am not a poet.
description: "Boyce" left home for the first time in September of 1911, and came to Clemson. When he arrived, many of the boys were puzzled to know if he was "Mutt" from Panama or "Ichabod Crane." They soon found that he was neither of the above notable characters, but was "Mutt" from Honea Path instead. "Mutt's" genial disposition has gained for him many friends, and we may well expect a great future for this "long-fellow."

name: Moore, George Franklin
clubs: Literary Critic, Debater Annual Celebration Columbian Literary Society
Secretary and Treasurer Senior Privates Club
description: He is a product of Florence. With his ready wit, his love of fun, and his willingness to help others even before he helps himself, he has become a general favorite. He amuses himself by making one hundred on "Dicky," or writing jokes on his friends in the columns of The Tiger. He can capture an innocent man or offer up a fervent prayer with equal grace. He's too busy to study, yet his record will compare favorably with the best. Ease of language is his. Backed by a ready wit and a kindly heart, that seeks to amuse, not to wound.

name: Moore, Javan Sylvester
motto: Keep time. How sour sweet music is, When time is broke, and no proportion kept!
description: Blew the fuses of the Electric City, Anderson, and landed at Clemson, with his trombone in one hand and an old boy's suitcase in the other, to study about animals. Although considered a good fellow, he has been sliding his way through school on his trombone. He joined hands with the Glee Club, and finally led them on a tour. Good nature and pleasant disposition have won him many friends; and we all wish him much success.

name: Monroe, James Beasley
motto: "Modesty is the graceful, calm virtue of maturity; bashfulness, the charm of vivacious youth."
description: He comes from the famous swamp of Marion County. He honored Clemson with his presence in 1911. He is a real artist with the ladies. Unless he gets three letters a day, he declares that all the girls have "kicked" him. He has made many friends here by his quiet, honest, easy disposition. His only failing is his love for study (?). We feel sure of his success, as he is likely to revolutionize the agriculture of the world.

name: Muldrow, Henry Winston
motto: Woman makes man ambitious
description: He is a cute, good-natured fellow, whose great winning ways have won for him the best regards of his classmates. He is a theorist, and an expounder of fire-coals and frogs is he. Combining his military aspirations, soil theories, etc., he bids fair to astonish the world. Small in stature, large in ideas and heart, he has the world at his feet—that is his part of it—the soil. That "PIE" will succeed we feel sure.

name: Murray, Joseph Jefferson
motto: To be good, rather than to be conspicuous
clubs: Reporting Critic, Literary Critic, Chairman Invitation Committee, Marshal at Annual Celebration Palmetto Literary Society
description: In the fall of 1911, this youth migrated to Clemson from St. George; but now he hails from historic old Abbeville. He was never ambitious for military honors, but he has some of the highest thrust upon him. He is a loyal member of the "Bull" section. While in College, he has shown much pluck and ability, and we predict nothing but success for him.

name: Norris, John Edward
motto: "Let the doors be shut upon him, that he may play the fool nowhere but in his own home."
description: In 1910, "Prep" left home to see the world. He was lured off the train by the word "Clemson." After wandering for several hours, he found Clemson. He then decided to be a soldier boy. He was taken sick, and went home. However, he was not dismayed, but was back in September. He is an authority on soils, and will probably show those Orangeburg "mossbacks" how to convert a sandy desert into a fertile plain.

name: Osborne, Fred
motto: A man can be whatever he determines to be
description: He is a "Fair Representative of His Home Town," for he has been very successful during his four years of College life, and numbers his friends by the score. He stands in with the ladies, with the Colonel, and with "Dicky." Call on him when a man is needed to carry a wounded "Rat" to the hospital. "FRITZ" will probably drop Chemistry, and in a few years will be heard from as a rising Major in the United States Army.

A man can be whatever he determines to be
He is a "Fair Representative of His Home Town," for he has been very successful during his four years of College life, and numbers his friends by the score. He stands in with the ladies, with the Colonel, and with "Dicky." Call on him when a man is needed to carry a wounded "Rat" to the hospital. "FRITZ" will probably drop Chemistry, and in a few years will be heard from as a rising Major in the United States Army.

name: Osbocratoglovo, Josiah Thomas
motto: I came; I saw; I conquered
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Sophomore Dancing Club
description: This young man is known by his friends as "FRITZ-PUD-GRATE." He has achieved many military feats, which have been duly rewarded. The war game is his favorite pastime. Among his curio collection are numerous ringlets of stiff hair, which he prizes highly. He can be seen walking on the campus, as if he owned the entire College, when he is not studying Military Science. We predict for him a great military career.

motto: "Some live to eat; I live to swear"
clubs: Recording Secretary, Literary Critic, President Calhoun Literary Society
description: "Efford" is a man of whom Bishopville should feel justly proud. Not only does he excel as a model student, but he is an all-round College leader in all that is for the best. "Efford" made a financial success of The Tiger; and as a man of strict business principles we predict for him great success in life, whether it be in raising more and better pork and cattle, or in the administration of justice at the White House.

motto: A laddie jolly, gay, and hale, From world-renowned Allendale.

name: Peep, Julian Wiley
motto: Laugh, and the world laughs with you; weep, and you weep alone
description: From the wilderness of Wide-awake, came to Clemson in '10. He himself says that the inhabitants of that place are wild, and it is astonishing how "Peep" has improved himself since coming out of the mire. Much to the dismay of his friends, he still sticks to "the good old ways" of his ancestors. "Miss Peep" has made himself noted as a peddler of McCrackin's old stock of pinder candy. "Peep" often tells us of his lassie, who he says is devoted to him.

name: Poore, Ralph Dugas
motto: 'Tis worth that makes the man
description: This fellow made his appearance at Clemson in the fall of 1911, with the object of enlightening the world along Agricultural lines. He, being a good student and a hard worker, has succeeded admirably. He also has taken much interest in Literary Society work, which after graduation will help him, when he returns home, to be a farm demonstrator and a judge of cattle. We feel sure that with all his energy and endurance he will make a success of whatever he undertakes.

name: Ray, Neal Benjamin
motto: Start right; aim right; and keep pecking away
clubs: Vice-President, Critic, Recording Secretary, Sergeant-at-Arms, Calhoun Literary Society
description: He is a native of Greenwood. He came to Clemson to do things, and since his arrival here he has taken an active part in Literary Society work and other College activities. He is a fellow who does not fear work, being the possessor of a large quantity of stick-to-itiveness. His quiet, unassuming manner and genial disposition have won for him many friends, who expect great things of this lad in the future.

name: Bennett, Carl Ganson
motto: Get there first; and let no man beat you
description: In the fall of 1911, he arrived at College, and casting his lot with his fellow-Freshmen he has been true to them ever since. He does not believe in procrastination, and when a task is before him he will not stop till he has finished it. His sterling qualities have won for him many friends, and he is sure to succeed in life.

name: Ragsdale, William Glenn
motto: Darling, I am growing old
clubs: Sergeant-at-Arms, Marshal Annual Celebration Palmetto Literary Society
description: "Gutz" is an innocent-looking lad; but those that know him say that he deceives his looks. "Gutz" has made many friends since entering Clemson. His greatest ambition is to invent a machine to pick the knots out of threads. If successful, he expects to take unto himself a helpmate. We predict a great success in the textile world for "Gutz."

name: Randle, Edwin Lowman
motto: Not that I love military honors less, but my friends more.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Behold Him! This second Ford Sterling, this original wit, and whatever you will! is the joke artist of the Class. No man dare dispute his right. However, he has several diversions: among them, electricity, football, 4-5 p.m. classes in Military Science, and others. Beneath his fun is a lovable nature, and a real purpose in life. If doesn't lead the Keystone Comedy Company, he will some day become famous as the designer of magnetos for lightning bugs.

name: Richards, Stephen Malone
motto: "To know, to esteem, to love—and then to part, Makes up life's tale to many a feeling heart."
description: Liberty Hill has one "watermelon" of whom she is justly proud. He takes things as they come, and usually makes the most of them. His ability as manager shows signs of an entrepreneur; and he should be congratulated on the financial success of this book. "Dick" is popular here with both sexes, and if he doesn't win him a home in Greenville he will probably go back to the "hills" and show the farmers how to raise "more watermelons and better watermelons."

name: Rosa, Joseph Tooker
motto:
"Onrushing torrents strive in vain
This hero's glory to restrain"
description: The hero of the Apple Valley expedition, is a conspicuous "Palm Beacher," and has already begun a career as writer and authority on horticultural subjects. If a man is from Georgetown, he is "JOE'S" friend forever. Eventually "JOE" will turn the Waccamaw marshes into beautiful gardens. Is "Boss" in the Norfolk crew. He is immune to bacillus colonel-cuniculi. Has been in more firms without becoming bankrupt than any other man ever heard of. "JOE" can finance his affairs, whether the war is on or not.

description: "MAJOR," as he is known among his friends, hails from the vicinity of Marion, S. C. "MAJOR" has made many friends among the boys, as well as among the Faculty. His cheery, good disposition wins him favor wherever he goes. However, his greatest conquest has been made among the girls, and some of the girls' greatest conquests have been made against "MAJOR." We predict great success for him in his research work, which will probably be in the Philippines.

motto: The whisper of a beautiful woman can be heard farther than the loudest call of duty.
description: He admits that he "is a fair representative of his home town," that being the noted "Richburg." He is very popular among his College mates, and from all indications he is also popular at "Winthrop." He is taking the Agronomy course, and intends to return to his "native brier-patch" and demonstrate to his fellow farmers how to raise two umbrellas per acre where only one had formerly been raised.

name: Pettus, Holmes
motto: "SWEETHEART" comes from Silverstreet, in the land of Cotton Bales, though he is not patriotic enough to support him.
clubs: Recording Secretary, Literary Critic Calhoun Literary Society
description: He stands high in the estimation of his professors, and when he leaves they shall "sigh for the touch of a vanished hand, and the sound of a voice that is still." By his energetic superintendence, he has brought the Agricultural Journal into prominence among college publications. He has that ideal disposition that makes friends anywhere. "We must laugh before we are happy, for fear we may die before we laugh at all."

name: Shannon, Charles John
motto: Little, but loud
description: Claims Camden as his place of abode. Is small in size only, as has been shown by his enviable record since entering Clemson. Has always excelled in every branch—from academic duties to love. Although not inclined to a military life, his natural ability has secured for him high offices. He is widely known as the author of "Spooning," and evidently has good experience in this line. A successful career awaits him in the textile world.

name: Shealy, Arthur Liston
motto: "While ascending the hill of Prosperity, may I never meet a friend."
clubs: Lieutenant and Battalion Quartermaster and Commissary
description: "Mouse" hails from that particular corner of Newberry County known as "Frog Level." "Mouse" is a most fortunate specimen of humanity, in that he has acquired by environment quite a musical tone of voice, which is in full harmony with the croaking of those songsters who were his faithful entertainers during many happy days which he spent at "Frog Level." Being a prospective doctor, "heart-soothing" will be his specialty. "Near-home practice" is his motto; "thirty-page prescriptions" his hobby.

name: Simmons, Benjamin Francis
motto: He would cut off his nose to spite his face.
description: He is little in stature, but big in heart. The little characteristic has won him many friends. "BENNIE" is somewhat shy of ladies in public, but Tuesday always brings him a letter with Cupid's stamp on it. His chief occupation is going to the store, and smoking cigarettes. His sincerity and ability are sure to win him a place in this world.

motto: Never trouble trouble until trouble troubles you.
description: He is one of those rare degenerates from Richburg. He has fallen into the line of evolution by the acquisition of a host of friends since he entered Clemson. He is never troubled by the incongruities of this life, but rather takes things easy. He has often thought of going to the Philippines, but has finally decided that he cannot walk quite so far as easily as he walked seven miles to tell that fair lady good bye.

name: Smith, Joe Major
motto: If the world's wealth lay at my feet, I would invest it in Reveille Tickets.
description: The personification of good nature and good looks—rejoicing in the euphonious cognomen of "JOE MAJOR," from the twinkling city of Starr. "JOE," with his splendid repartee and unfailing good humor, has won for himself an enviable place in the hearts of his classmates. He is the luckiest man in the Senior Class; therefore, we feel safe in predicting that Hartwell, Ga., will soon capitulate, and a cotton mill president will subscribe himself as "JOE MAJOR SMITH."

motto: "His only books were women's looks; And folly's all they taught him."
clubs: Publication Committee A. I. E. E.
Delegate S. C. C. P. A.
description: Yes, girls, this is "MARY." Did he derive that name from those rosy cheeks, and that girlish smile? We do not know. He brought that name from "Pudden Swamp." Don't be mistaken in a name. "MARY" is a man of ability. He can finance anything from The Chronicle to the S. C. C. P. A. As for the girls, they can't resist him. "MARY" not only attracts the girls, but is one of the most popular men in College.

clubs: Sole Possessor of the Mighty Pen
description: "Pat," as he is known by everyone, hails from the "city" of Pendleton. He has a heart that is big and true, and a sunny smile that will never wear off. Talk about handling the Shovel; he is right there with the goods along any line. "Pat" has already won a home in Pendleton, but says he expects to go to the Philippines after graduation, and that he will buy a ticket for two.

We predict for a great success in life.

clubs: Debater at Annual Celebration of Society '15
Secretary and Vice President, Dr. Riggs' Bible Class
description: "Doc," as he is well known, landed at Clemson in the fall of 1911, with the determination of enlightening the world along electric lines. In this he has succeeded, as is evidenced by his record of the past four years. "Doc's" original ideas and inventive abilities have won for him the admiration and appreciation of his friends. While "Doc" may invent perpetual motion, we predict that he will surprise the engineers of the General Electric Company with his direct current generator without a commutator.

Secretary and Treasurer Carlisle Bible Class
description: Behold our midget Captain, who migrated from Enoree. Not only does "STRIB" rule "A" Company, but "The" ladies. The only way to secure friends is to be one, is a policy which "STRIB" has kept, and which has secured him scores of friends. description: His two characteristics are his ability to handle the "shovel," and to laugh longer and louder than anybody else. "SARGE" possesses an abundance of good nature, and takes things as they come, especially fruit-cake. He is some "Ladies' Man," spending a great deal of time writing; and dancing like a lightning bug. "SARGE" has plenty of ability, and can shoot well and hard when he feels like it. We predict that Laurens will be proud of this lad's success in the future.

clubs: Executive Committee S. C. C. P. A.
description: We have, embodied in this tall Westerner, the rare combination of genius and hard work. He is one of the few who have allowed study to interfere with his College course. His effective "shooting," as well as his good-natured smile and congenial manner, have won for him the respect and admiration of Faculty and students. "SWINE" excels everyone in the boxing ring or speaking upon the rostrum. He is at home in a box-car, or gracing the head of a banquet table. "SWINE" is a born leader of men.

clubs: Recording Secretary, Reporting Critic, Vice-President Columbia Literary Society
Vice-President, Secretary and Treasurer Greenwood County Club
description: The "Son" of Greenwood is the "Sun" of our Class. He is noted for the fact that he is always in a good humor. He is most especially so when he has just made a home-run. We all expect great things from "LITTLE TIGER." Some day he will be playing center-field for the "Giants" in the summer, and singing bass (?) in vaudeville in the winter.

description: It has been hinted that "George" snuggled himself into barracks in his big brother's coat pocket, even if he did come from Pomaria. "George" is a combination of Napoleon and Steinmetz. He leads his marine company through storm and sunshine, and knows how to wire—home for money. "George" won his fame as a soldier by mobilizing some famous Senior Privates. He has convinced us that he is there when it comes to arguing—by keeping us awake till the early hours. His many friends wish him much success for his future.

Besides being the best musician, he is one of the best in that electricity stuff, and also one of the best-liked fellows in the whole school. We predict for him the best of success in his chosen line of work - Electricity; or in whatever he may decide to follow.

name: Teal, William Archibald
motto: "Greater love hath no man than this, That he lay down his Second Lieutenancy for his friends."
clubs: Reporting Critic, Censor, Vice-President Palmetto Literary Society
description: Drifted over to Clemson from Hartsville, in the fall of '11. The following summer, he won great fame and his nickname by capturing a wild elephant in the jungles of Darlington County. This dangerous exploit was set forth in the pages of the "Darlington Jungle Enlightener." His ready smile and friendly ways have won for him a host of friends. If he continues to work as diligently in the future as during his College life, his success is certain.

name: Cope, S. C.
motto: Of all the wonders that I yet have heard, it seems to me most strange that men should fear
description: He decided early in life that the height of his ambition was to be a farmer. Therefore, he journeyed to Clemson in 1911, and has stuck with the Class through thick and thin ever since. He specialized in "Hutch"; and, if reports are true, he ought to know something about cotton. He is noted for his fearless, straightforward nature. He is that type of man who makes good. name: Thornton, Robert Pressly
motto: "One gentle form and fair, Beneath whose smile to bask, And learn love's sweetness there"
clubs: Cor. Sec'y, Vice-Pres. Calhoun Literary Society
Sec'y and Treas., Vice-Pres. Laurens County Club
Asst. Ed.-in-Chief, Editor Chemistry Dept. Agricultural Journal
Orator Annual Celebration Calhoun Literary Society
description: "Al," as he is generally known, hails from Mountville, S. C. His accomplishments are many, and especially is he overloaded with achievements on the athletic field; yet he is the same old boy, with a cheery good word for all his friends. He is very fond of chocolate cake and frequent letters; and a certain sweet little girl always sees that he gets them. Instead of following Chemistry, "Al," will probably go home and marry the "sweet little girl."

name: Todd, James Gordon
motto: What will I do? I'll dam the Amazon and reinforce the North Pole.
description: Behold a product of our neighboring town, Seneca. Came to Clemson to make an engineer of himself, but since then, having become connected with the Exchange, he has about given up that notion. He likes the lunch counter, one of his particular sandwiches being "Ham." Is a quiet, agreeable fellow, who possesses the good opinion of everybody. Is sure to be heard from in the future by his classmates and the world.

name: Trescot, John Holmes
motto: Not that I love military honors less, but that I love my friends more.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer, Anderson County Club '14
Secretary and Treasurer, Junior Dancing Club '14
Secretary and Treasurer, Senior Dancing Club '15
description: List, gentle reader, here is the will power of his noble aggregation. hit a little streak of hard luck in his Senior year, and had to be a "jailbird" for a few months. There is not the slightest doubt that is out in search of knowledge, but he goes about so quietly that we ignorant mortals never derive any benefit from his search. He is sure to succeed in this cold world, and we feel sure that some of that knowledge will leak out of his lofty bean.

name: Trotter, Augustus Massenburg
motto: On their own merits modest men are dumb
clubs: Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: This young scion of old Camden came to Clemson with the double purpose of getting an education and making money. The number of big words he loves to overwhelm us with gives proof that our Prof's efforts have not been wasted; and his prosperous appearance indicates ample success in the financial line too. presents the ideal combination of being an earnest Y. M. C. A. worker, and a Society man, which is too seldom found; and more honor be to him for such fine qualities.

name: Vaughan, Claude Lawrence
motto: A man's worth is never shown by his military aspirations
description: It is real hard to say just where this "good-looking, black-haired" lad came from, but he claims Darlington as his "brier-patch." Since he entered College, he has made quite a reputation among the students and Faculty, and it was hard for him to decide on his course: but after consulting the Colonel and Professor Daniel he decided to specialize on English and Military Science. He expects to cast his lot with the native Filipinos, and make himself famous teaching the natives of the island the art of Agriculture.

name: Wannamaker, Herbert Lee
motto: "Cheer up! If you are blue, get color blind and forget it."
description: This lusty lad hails from the town of St. Matthews. Having decided to view the world through a transit, he came to Clemson to inform himself in the "Hobo" Course. His efforts have been very great, but his accomplishments very few, though very accurate and promising; thus showing the brilliancy of the chap's ability. The future is very promising for "WANNA."

name: Wallace, Bruce Waxham
motto: "If I have freedom in my love, And in my soul am free, Angels alone that soar above enjoy such liberty."
description: In Calhoun County has a son of whom to be proud—keen in intellect, studious in habits, standing for all that is good and for the betterment of life. Some day will make Aeolian Hill, of Calhoun, as widely known in that county's agriculture as the aeolian harp is known in history. After four years of diligent work, is prepared to make his commencement in the battle of life. A bright, prosperous future is predicted for him.

name: Ward, Josh
motto: "His life is gentle, and the elements So mixed in him that Nature might stand up And say to all the world, 'This is a man'!"
clubs: Censor, Chairman Query Committee, Pin Committee Calhoun Literary Society
description: This smiling youth hails from Georgetown. He came to Clemson in '10 with the full intention of astonishing the world, and with that end in view took the Electrical Course. has a quiet disposition, and is a good companion, never leading his friends astray. After finishing the Electrical Course at Clemson, intends to study patent law. Unless the call of "Big Six" becomes too great to resist, we predict for him unlimited success in this, his chosen profession.

name: Watson, David Joseph
motto: "The wind it blows, the vessel goes, But where and whither no one knows"
description: Call him "Duck," and he will smile; but it is as "Pete" we know him best. Never depressed in spirits, he takes life as it comes, brightening those around him with his cheery smiles and ready wit. Though he lost much time during his course, he has by his optimism and perseverance remained true to his Class. It is his chiefest ambition to find the ideal of his dreams, and then become mayor and political leader of Watson's Siding.

name: Wood, John Terry Jr.
motto: I'd rather be a live coward than a dead hero
description: He enlisted into the Freshman Class in 1911, and has remained loyal to the Class of '15. His chief hobby is to argue about some great political question; and his only defect is that he thinks of but one thing at a time. His ability as a textile man is something of which to be proud, and we expect to hear from him in the future textile world.

name: Bigby, Walter Asbury
motto: To know him is to like him; Have I not said enough?
clubs: Corresponding Secretary, Center, Chaplain, President Calhoun Literary Society
description: "WALT" arrived upon the scene in September, 1911, and being very much fascinated by a look through a transit he immediately joined the "Hobo" crew. "WALT's" straightforwardness and fine carriage have achieved for him a notable military career. He is especially fond of the girls and poetry. His popularity and many friends confirm the fact that he is an all-around good fellow. With all his frankness, good nature, and jovial spirit, we see nothing less than a bright future in store for him.

name: Blake, William Ernest
motto: To strive, to seek, to find, and not to yield
description: Leaving the Methodists, at Wofford, blew into our midst in the Junior year to take the Electrical Course, and since has been a faithful member of this Class. Though burdened with make-up work, he found time for the Literary Societies, in which he has taken a prominent part. He also pilots the A. I. E. E. is not a sport; yet we know him to be a great admirer of the fair sex. It is a sure bet that, with his deep, firm set determination, he will make good.

name: Bostick, Benjamin
description: A young man of twenty-one, has just entered the Clermont Agricultural Society, and has made many friends. His greatest ambition is to become a farmer, and he has already succeeded in the undertaking. His grace and deportment make him a great favorite, especially among the ladies. With his great energy and character, we predict for him unlimited success in the future.

name: Bristol, Harold Whitman
motto: "His life is gentle, and the elements So mixed in him that Nature might stand up And say to all the world, 'This is a man.'"
description: He entered College with the intention of making good, and has succeeded in everything to which he has turned his hand. At home and at College he has always been a leader. He is a man of experience, having tried everything from stoking on a gasoline tug to leading German figures in a ballroom. That he will reach the top in his profession is the belief of all who know him.

name: Franklin, Lewis Bunker
motto: Who wired Ensley?
clubs: Corresponding Secretary, President Columbian Literary Society
description: And this is he—a veritable bunch of energy; a man of many moods and turns. "Tim" has touched upon almost everything. His specialties are electricity, steam, movies, music, poetry, art, etc. He is also a hypnotist and magician. He has by his genial disposition won his way into the hearts of all the fellows. Though "Tim" leads a busy life, he always finds time for his friends, and his "lady fair." We predict for him great success in whatever line he follows.

name: Buyck, David Derrick Jr.
motto: Shoot 'ducks' electrically
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer, Calhoun County Club
description: As his friends call him, hails from the city of St. Matthews. From a scared "Rat" to a "Senior Private," ambitions have been to make good; and he has succeeded. Takes great pride in his work, and has been most successful. He has won many friends, who are true to him. If can do as well in life as he does in shooting ducks and data, he is sure to succeed in this world.

name: Burnette, George Neal
motto: "I was not born for courts or great affairs; I pay my debts, believe, and say my prayers."
description: "G. N.," as he is commonly called, received a "Dip" from Furman in 1913. Being unusually bright, he entered the Junior Class, and he expects to get another "dip" in June, with the Class of 1915. "G. N.," as he is sometimes called, arose from the ranks to a first lieutenancy by inventing new commands in military tactics. His sole aim in life is to stick to a thing until he gets it. With this aim in life, we predict a brilliant future for him.
